behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module wiring check (3 collectors + aggregator + summary input contract). A unit test of one function can miss field-mapping and merge bugs between modules, while no real external infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with test doubles/fake local adapters for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log returning distinct sentinel values; assert the exact payload handed to the summary step contains all three source sections and expected key fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in orchestration-level error handling and partial-report assembly. Pure unit tests can miss exception propagation and section-level fallback behavior across module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline with two sources returning valid data and one source adapter raising a realistic fetch exception/timeout error; assert process completes, report is generated, and only the failed section contains `data unavailable` while other sections are preserved.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: This is a quality/correctness claim about generated language output. If summary generation uses a real LLM, correctness depends on model behavior and can vary run-to-run; mocked generation would hide real hallucination/misstatement failures.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed input datasets with known key values into the real summary-generation model/API, run multiple trials, and score outputs against required factual assertions (e.g., numeric values, trend direction, top incidents). Fail if factual accuracy rate drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real networked email delivery/inbox acceptance and full process execution. In-process mocks can pass even when SMTP/API auth, transport, or message formatting breaks in production.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against reachable test instances/services and a real test mailbox; verify an email is received by configured recipients with expected subject/body and report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This spans process runs, filesystem persistence, and transient external service failure/recovery. Mocking delivery often hides real retry-path failures (connection errors, persisted artifact format/path, second-run resend behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute script with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report file is persisted locally with retry metadata. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation simulation): restore email service, execute script again, assert persisted report is retried/sent successfully and retry artifact is cleared/marked sent.
